meta.name: Tomato Basil Soup
meta.subtitle: Simple, popular comfort soup • perfect with grilled cheese

source.notes: SSA-friendly baseline. Supports pantry tomatoes, fresh tomatoes, dairy or dairy-free finish, and batch/freezer workflows.

yield.notes: Makes ~10–12 cups depending on simmer and blending.

tasteProfile.notes: Tomato-forward, aromatic basil, optional cream for richness, optional sugar/honey to balance acidity.

ingredients.label: Olive oil or butter
ingredients.notes: About 1 tablespoon. Use butter for richer flavor.

ingredients.label: Crushed tomatoes (canned)
ingredients.notes: 2 (14–15 oz) cans, or 1 large (28 oz) can.

ingredients.label: Vegetable broth (or water + bouillon), low-sodium preferred
Salt
ingredients.notes: Start with ~1 teaspoon; adjust at end based on broth.

ingredients.label: Fresh basil leaves
ingredients.notes: About 1 loosely packed cup. Add at end to preserve aroma.

ingredients.label: Heavy cream (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 cup. For creamy tomato basil soup.

ingredients.label: Whole milk (optional alternative to cream)
ingredients.notes: About 3/4 cup. Warm gently; avoid hard boil.

steps.title: Sweat aromatics
instructions: Heat oil (or butter) in a pot over medium heat.
Add onion (and carrot if using). Cook 6–8 minutes until softened and lightly translucent.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Build tomato base
instructions: If using tomato paste, stir it in and cook 1 minute to deepen flavor.
Add crushed tomatoes, broth, bay leaf (optional), salt, and pepper.

steps.title: Simmer
instructions: Simmer 20–25 minutes, partially covered, stirring occasionally.
Taste: if tomatoes are very acidic, add a little sugar/honey (optional).

steps.title: Blend and add basil
instructions: Blend until smooth using an immersion blender (or carefully blend in batches in a countertop blender).
Stir in fresh basil and blend briefly (or finely chop basil and stir in for flecks).

steps.title: Optional creamy finish and final seasoning
instructions: For creamy soup, lower heat and stir in cream, milk, or coconut milk (choose one).
Warm gently—avoid a hard boil after adding dairy.
Taste and adjust salt, pepper, and sweetness. Serve hot.

bestPractices: For best freezing, freeze the soup before adding dairy; add cream/milk when reheating.
Cool quickly and portion into flat freezer bags or containers for efficient stacking.

useWhatYouHaveLogic.notes: If no fresh basil: use 1–2g dried basil added during simmer. If no broth: use water + bouillon; season at the end.

storage.fridge: Refrigerate up to 5 days. If dairy added, reheat gently.
storage.freezer: Freeze up to 3 months. Prefer freezing without dairy; add dairy on reheat.
storage.reheat: Reheat to a gentle simmer, stirring often; thin with broth/water if needed.

notes: Tomato quality varies; if the soup tastes flat, add a pinch more salt and a small splash of vinegar or lemon juice (optional) for lift.
For extra richness without dairy, blend in 30–60g cooked white beans (optional idea) — keep in mind this is not listed as an ingredient in this baseline.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions. No disallowed 'toppings' property used.
